entry.title: Sydney Morning Herald: 19 Jul, 2022
entry.summary: Sydney Morning Herald: 19 Jul, 2022 published at 2022-07-19 07:35AM.
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Age no barre as seniors reach out at ballet class
  • ‘Perfect match’: Gladys Berejiklian on life after politics and her new job at Optus
  • ‘Extremely disappointed’: Mirvac’s AMP office fund raid draws fire
  • New rules would stop Liberals ‘carving up seats like in Lawrence of Arabia’
  • Cashed up and employed: RBA says households can withstand higher rates

  • Scottish Premiership player to head south after signing with Perth Glory

  • Coastal erosion warning as NSW prepares for strong winds and swell
  • Queenwood principal to head 640-year-old British boys’ school

  • ‘Vertical firecracker’: Boris Johnson takes to the skies as contenders to replace him battle below

  • Collateral damage: China, Sri Lanka and a developing debt crisis

  • McGowan urges parents to mask kids at school but won’t get ‘heavy-handed’ yet

  • Pop artist made giant impression with sculptures of everyday items
  • Toddlers closer to receiving COVID vaccine, government says supply ‘still needs to be arranged’
  • Qld police search for witness of Fortitude Valley station stabbing

  • The Wrap: ASX dips as tech falls counter buoyant energy stocks
  • Europe’s future set to be decided in the next few days
  • War for talent myth debunked
  • JB Hi-Fi posts record sales on back of online boom
  • BHP braces for rising costs, labour crunch in ‘volatile’ year ahead
  • Wartime CEO: Mark Zuckerberg’s ruthlessness is what Facebook needs now
  • The insider trader out of jail and on the hunt for his next big deal
  • Don’t be fooled: Good economic stories often sound like bad news
  • The Warren Buffett protege doing her own thing
  • ‘Project Galileo’: Inside ANZ’s $4.9 billion play for Suncorp’s bank
  • Meshel Laurie slams The Project for ‘token’ use of Indigenous guests

  • Forget cancel culture, avoid Johnny Depp’s new album because it’s bad
  • Power and polish: Anna Wintour reigns in a gossipy tour of the elite
  • ‘The bogans were adorable’: Miriam Margolyes on burnouts and her new TikTok fame
  • Rampant ‘cultural theft’ of Indigenous art to be tackled with new laws
  • The five graphics that explain the State of the Environment report

Breaking news from Sydney, Australia and the world. Features the latest business, sport, entertainment, travel, lifestyle, and technology news.

entry.title: South China Morning Post: 19 Jul, 2022
entry.summary: South China Morning Post: 19 Jul, 2022 published at 2022-07-19 06:53AM.
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • 3 Hong Kong jobseekers lose $1.1 million to scammers after falling victim to online shopping con
  • ‘Anti-China clampdown’ remarks by top state official on Hong Kong affairs not targeting difference in views, Beijing heavyweight says
  • Second man arrested in Hong Kong over case of 23 unauthorised alerts from TVB app
  • More Hong Kong students pick Britain for further education as pound sinks to record low
  • China’s global yuan goals aided by RCEP trade deal, Hong Kong’s role as ‘super connector’, top banker says
  • Banks, restaurants and cafes swoop in as Hong Kong’s retail rental index sinks to a 34-year low
  • Coronavirus: outbreaks at Hong Kong public hospitals grow, as John Lee warns quarter of healthcare resources could be used up by end of the month
  • Hong Kong expects scorching temperatures, coinciding with arrival of ‘Great Heat’ day on Saturday
  • Hongkongers send 400 million disposable coffee cups to landfills every year, according to green group’s estimates
  • World War II bomb scare forces evacuation of Hong Kong construction sites, petrol stations, but suspect device later found to be harmless
  • Complaints over Hong Kong food delivery services increase, claims of potentially ‘misleading’ sales practices
  • Five arrested, cannabis and suspected cocaine seized after separate operations at Hong Kong flat and car stop
  • Coronavirus: patients quarantining at home in Hong Kong made to wait for compulsory electronic wristbands; 3,436 cases logged
  • Manila wants to revisit Chinese loans for 3 rail projects as analysts warn of ‘risks’
  • US House Speaker Nancy Pelosi reported to visit Taiwan in August after Covid postponed April trip
  • EU lawmaker opens Taiwan visit with vow to ‘stand firm’ with Taipei
  • China’s military puts advanced rocket launch system to the test at high altitude
  • Coronavirus: Chinese city says sorry for break-in drive to find close contacts
  • China bond defaults hit US$20 billion in 2022, more than double last year’s total, as property developers teeter
  • China’s flagging GDP growth just tip of the iceberg for size of disruptions, amount of losses from zero-Covid-19 policy
  • Is strained China-US relationship spiralling towards a Thucydides Trap scenario?
  • Chinese national in racist video scandal charged with human trafficking in Malawi
  • China, South Korea tech competition and US-led bloc have Seoul scrambling to prevent leaks but protect trade
  • US Senate to vote on China semiconductor bill to increase competitiveness, boost industry
  • New Yorkers Sherry Li and Lianbo Wang charged with funnelling Chinese investors’ money to Donald Trump campaign
  • Economic boon or security threat? American city torn over Chinese milling project
  • China GDP: why red-hot inflation could slow Beijing’s bid to unseat the US as world’s No 1 economy
  • Chinese scientists simulate hypersonic flight to US after devising BeiDou satellite-switching system
  • As China’s youth unemployment keeps rising, Beijing says regional governments need to step up
  • Chinese minister’s trip to US a sign of collaboration on climate and a hint of hope for ties
  • China corruption: executive who oversaw country’s main semiconductor industry fund under investigation
  • China asks European leaders to meet Xi in November. But will they accept?
  • Wristbands, big data used to monitor people who commit ‘less serious’ crimes in China
  • Record-breaking Chinese bridge clears hurdle to faster transport in border province
  • Taiwan welcomes ‘loyal friend’ ex-US defence chief Mark Esper on four-day visit
  • TikTok owner ByteDance opens 31 chip design jobs, joining Big Tech rivals amid China’s self-sufficiency drive
  • Henan bank crisis savers must ‘wait patiently’ for compensation amid systemic risk, social instability concerns
  • Shinzo Abe’s death ‘could have been avoided’ if his security acted faster, new analysis shows
  • Australia’s ‘utter failure’ on conservation, environment exposed in ‘disturbing’ government report
  • As South Korea seeks to mend ties with Japan, forced labour victims fear a repeat of 2015’s ‘comfort women’ deal fiasco
  • The Australia-based start-up that beat Elon Musk’s Neuralink to the punch … 3 times
  • Indonesia’s Joko Widodo hints at how he wants to be remembered: through his budgets and Nusantara spending plan
  • Malaysia seizes US$18 million in elephant tusks, rhino horns and pangolin scales, possibly destined for China
  • Yellen says US allies must not allow China to dominate raw materials, technologies, and thwart ‘unfair trade practices’
  • Japan won’t cap defence spending amid increased ‘anxiety’ over Russia, China, North Korea
  • Pacific island nations call for World Court to rule on big polluters’ obligations
  • UK’s Boris Johnson takes back seat, enjoys the ride during final weeks in power
  • Donald Trump eyes early launch to 2024 White House run, casting shadow over November midterms
  • NRA and police hail customer who shot dead gunman in Indiana shopping centre attack
  • US developing satellites to track Chinese and Russian hypersonic weapons
  • Zelensky purges Ukraine’s secret service after ousting childhood friend as top spy
  • US judge broke promise not to jail Roman Polanski for sex abuse, newly unsealed testimony reveals
  • Rishi Sunak widens lead in race to become next UK prime minister
  • Biden’s Medal of Freedom picks a reminder of US lack of regard for Asian-Americans
  • Top US Covid-19 adviser Anthony Fauci expects to retire by end of Joe Biden’s current term
  • ‘Runway has melted’: UK’s largest air force base and London Luton airport halt flights as heatwave bakes Britain
  • Ukraine war: Russian shelling kills 6 in Donbas; Vladimir Putin and Türkiye’s Erdogan to discuss grain exports
  • US, UK sexual health clinics lacking monkeypox care after years of oversight, inadequate funding
  • WHO declares Marburg outbreak in Ghana, after first two cases of deadly Ebola-like virus reported
  • Britain, France brace for hottest day as Europe fires rage
  • School district home rents drop by the most in 2 years as more Hong Kong residents head for the emigration queue
  • Henan bank crisis threatens to derail China’s efforts to maintain a stable yuan
  • Retailing in the metaverse: Metamall brings new thrills and challenges to physical stores in Hong Kong
  • Apple joins fellow tech giants in putting a lid on hiring amid recession fears
  • Hong Kong stocks slide as Apple parts suppliers, Macau casino operators suffer on earnings pressure
  • How higher inflation and rising interest rates will test Asia’s buoyant property market
  • Swire Pacific buys Coca-Cola’s bottling business in Vietnam and Cambodia for US$1 billion
  • Stellantis to end Jeep production in China after failing to take control of GAC joint venture
  • A fifth of Chinese developers face insolvency as investors grow tired of repeated bond extensions, S&P Global warns
  • Bel-Air villa in Hong Kong’s Pok Fu Lam fetches a record price as ultra-wealthy buyer pounces on foreclosed property
  • Goldman Sachs, HSBC, Ant among 20 institutions supporting initiative to nurture GBA fintech talent
  • Citigroup sees another 20 per cent upside in Chinese stocks this year on spending stimulus
  • Jam or cream first on scones? A top pastry chef’s verdict, a big lesson learned and his cravings for Hong Kong food in London
  • How to make sense of history when memories of an event differ subtly? Escape of Hong Kong POWs with help of a violinist is a case in point
  • How beating stage 4 lung cancer inspired a hotel manager to become a wellness tourism pioneer
  • Netflix K-drama review: Remarriage & Desires – matchmaking melodrama starring Kim Hee-seon and Lee Hyun-wook is slick enough, but lacks fireworks
  • 5 ancient Chinese instruments come alive in rousing Hong Kong concert inspired by China’s Dunhuang cave paintings
  • Cartier’s latest high jewellery collection combines creativity and timeless designs, says its style and heritage director Pierre Rainero
  • Baroque paintings, music and animation take art lovers in Hong Kong on an immersive journey
  • Which are the best countries offering citizenship by investment? 40 nations – from Thailand to the UAE – offer ‘golden visas’ to high net worth individuals splurging millions on property
  • Who were Ivana Trump’s 4 ex-husbands? She and Donald had kids Ivanka, Donald Jr. and Eric before he cheated with Marla Maples, but she also had a ‘Cold War marriage’ and a long-term ‘boy toy’ …
  • Elon Musk’s 6 biggest moneymakers, ranked: the billionaire may be in trouble with Twitter, but he’s got successful companies SpaceX, Tesla, Neuralink and The Boring Company bringing in over a trillion
  • Who was Elvis Presley’s only grandson, Benjamin Keough? Riley Keough’s brother, once the stepson of Michael Jackson while married to Lisa Marie, took his own life after struggling with addiction
  • Is Novak Djokovic the most eccentric man in tennis? He ate grass off the Wimbledon court after beating Nick Kyrgios, refuses to get his Covid-19 vaccination, and adores his poodle Pierre
  • Inside Chris Hemsworth’s superhero-worthy watch collection: the Thor actor dons Rolex, Bulgari, Audemars Piguet, and has two Tag Heuer timepieces – one seen in Netflix’s Spiderhead
  • Missing Princess Mako: what does Japan’s royal family think of her move to New York? Kako, Aiko and father Fumihito support her, but Japan’s PM Fumio Kishida is still not ready for a female emperor
SCMP.com, Hong Kong's premier online English daily provides exclusive up-to-date news, audio video news, podcasts, RSS Feeds, Blogs, breaking news, top stories, award winning news and analysis on Hong Kong and China.

entry.title: Asahi Shimbun: 19 Jul, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • All work and no play as scorching heat takes toll on kids’ activities

  • VOX POPULI: In mystifying weather, we wait for second end of rainy season
The Asahi Shimbun is widely regarded for its journalism as the most respected daily newspaper in Japan. The English version offers selected articles from the vernacular Asahi Shimbun, as well as extensive coverage of cool Japan,focusing on manga, travel and other timely news.
